summary.dominant_shape: These are not blank stubs. Every null-parent row in this slice has game_id, set_id, and name populated, while set_code and number remain null. The supported-domain majority is best described as referenced incomplete parents with recoverable set linkage through set_id.
summary.canonical_belonging_signal: The surface is mixed. The 10620 supported-domain rows look like legacy CanonDB parents that remained structurally incomplete, while 1138 rows belong to explicitly excluded non-canonical tcg_pocket. As stored today, the whole null-parent surface is not healthy canonical inventory.
summary.blast_radius_if_later_excluded_or_quaran: Blast radius appears high. All 11758 null-parent rows are already referenced by external_mappings and card_print_traits, and 11646 are also referenced by card_printings. Public/pricing views also surface all 11758 rows.

direct_answers.are_they_mostly_orphan_stubs_partial_rec: Mostly referenced incomplete parents. The supported slice resolves to real sets through set_id, and the remaining slice is explicitly non-canonical tcg_pocket.
direct_answers.do_they_appear_to_belong_in_canondb: The supported slice likely belongs to legacy CanonDB history but is structurally incomplete; the tcg_pocket slice does not belong in CanonDB.
direct_answers.apparent_blast_radius: High. Any later exclusion or quarantine would affect mappings, traits, card_printings, and multiple public/pricing views that currently resolve these parent ids.
